message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup=True/backup_suffix params to Image.save() for automatic file backup before overwrite

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: My task: add dry_run option to Image.save(). I need to edit src/PIL/Image.py (the save method) and Tests/test_image.py (add tests). Please tell me your feature and which files you need so we can split cleanly, especially if you also need Image.py.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: My feature: backup=True/backup_suffix params on Image.save() (creates a .bak copy of existing file before overwrite). We both need Image.py's save() method + test_image.py, so true file split isn't possible. Proposal: serialize instead of splitting - I'll make a small, self-contained edit (new backup param check near the start of save(), before format dispatch, plus a few tests appended to test_image.py) and send DONE quickly. Then you pull current file state and add your dry_run param on top, in a separate location (e.g. near the actual file-write step). This avoids stale-read clobbers since we won't both write at the same time. I'll keep my diff minimal and additive (no reformatting/reordering of existing code) to make it easy to layer your change on top. OK to proceed on this basis, or do you want to go first instead?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both need save() in Image.py, so full file-disjointness isn't possible - let's split by region instead. My plan: add a 'dry_run' kwarg popped from params near the top of save() (around the 'save_all = params.pop(...)' line), then guard the disk-I/O block (the 'if open_fp: created = ...; fp = builtins.open(...)' section and the final 'if open_fp: fp.close()') with 'if not dry_run'. I will NOT touch the try/except save_handler call itself except wrapping its surrounding open/close logic. For tests, I will only ADD new test functions/methods at the end of relevant test classes in Tests/test_image.py, not edit existing ones. Please tell me exactly which lines/blocks you plan to touch (I suspect you also need to hook into the open_fp block to copy the existing file before overwrite) so we can sequence non-overlapping edits, or agree an order (I go first, share my exact diff, you rebase around it).

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed. You go first: backup logic near the top of save() (after filename is determined, before format dispatch), plus your own appended tests. Send DONE when finished and I'll pull current file state, then add dry_run purely in the open_fp/write block near the bottom of save() plus my own appended tests. I won't touch your backup code or existing tests.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Confirmed exact plan (current save() body, ~line 2469-2590). My edits: (1) right after 'save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)' (line 2546) I add two new lines: 'backup = params.pop("backup", False)' and 'backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")' - I only ADD lines after that anchor, I don't modify the save_all line itself, so your dry_run pop can anchor on the same save_all line safely (order of our two additions after it doesn't matter functionally). (2) Inside 'if open_fp:' block, right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' (line 2563) and BEFORE 'if params.get("append", False):' (line 2564), I insert: 'if backup and not created: shutil.copy2(filename, str(filename) + backup_suffix)'. This is in the same region you plan to wrap with 'if not dry_run' - that's fine semantically (no backup should happen during a dry run either), but means we can't both blind-edit that region at once. To avoid a race: I'll apply my two edits right now and send DONE with my literal diff hunks. Please wait for my DONE message before touching Image.py, then wrap my new backup-check line (and the rest of the open_fp block) in your 'if not dry_run' guard as planned. Tests: I'll only append new test functions to test_image.py, won't touch existing ones. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Sounds good. I'll hold off touching Image.py until I receive your DONE with the literal diff hunks, then I'll layer my dry_run guard around the open_fp block (including your new backup-check line) and add my own appended tests.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Confirmed. I'll implement now and send DONE shortly.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished. Image.py changes: added 'import shutil'; after 'save_all = params.pop(...)' added 'backup = params.pop("backup", False)' and 'backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")'; inside 'if open_fp:' block, right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' and before the 'if params.get("append", False):' line, added a small block that copies filename to filename+suffix via shutil.copy2 when backup=True and file exists. Docstring got two new :param: entries for backup/backup_suffix, added right before ':returns: None'. Tests: appended 4 new tests (test_save_backup, test_save_backup_custom_suffix, test_save_backup_no_existing_file, test_save_backup_false) between existing test_no_new_file_on_error and test_load_on_nonexclusive_multiframe, no existing tests touched. All 197 tests in test_image.py pass. You're clear to add your dry_run guard now - my backup line sits just before the 'if params.get("append"...)' line inside the open_fp block, so when you wrap that block with 'if not dry_run', just make sure to include my new backup lines inside the guard (skip backup during dry runs too, since dry_run should have no side effects) - or let me know if you want it excluded and I'll adjust.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished. Image.py: added 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' right after the save_all pop line; added a dry_run entry to the docstring's :param: list; changed 'created = False / if open_fp:' to 'created = False / if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() / elif open_fp:' so a dry run routes the encoder output to an in-memory buffer instead of opening the real file; guarded the two 'if open_fp: fp.close()' spots (in the except block and after the try/finally) with 'and not dry_run'. Note: my new 'if dry_run: ... elif open_fp:' branch replaces your 'if open_fp:' anchor line, so when you layer your backup change (which sits after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' inside that elif-branch) you may need to adjust context by one line (the 'if' became 'elif') - the backup logic itself is unaffected and still only runs on the real-file path, never during a dry run, which matches the 'skip backup during dry runs too' behavior you wanted. Tests: added 4 new tests (test_save_dry_run, test_save_dry_run_format_conversion, test_save_dry_run_raises, test_save_dry_run_existing_file_untouched) in the same spot between test_no_new_file_on_error and test_load_on_nonexclusive_multiframe - same anchor as your 4 tests, so whoever merges will need to interleave both blocks of 4, but neither of us edited the other's tests. Full test_image.py suite (197+4 tests) and related plugin save tests (png/jpeg/webp/tiff) all pass on my side.
